humanSummary.text: Final re-audit found no P0/P1 blockers. Current API mutating routes and workflow calls are matrix-guarded, repository gates are covered, and branch draft permission divergence is resolved. Route-shape, skipped DB fixture visibility, aliasing, and repository identity hardening remain non-blocking follow-ups.

findings.title: API permission route discovery is tied to current routing shape
findings.description: The API mutation discovery guard recognizes the current top-level POST and projectRoute.resource switch style. Future nested control flow, route tables, helper predicates, or pathname switches could evade the matrix unless the routing style remains fixed.

evidence.detail: Final re-audit confirmed current mutating routes are covered, but the collector is intentionally pattern-limited.

findings.impact: A future API routing refactor could weaken permission-denial coverage without immediately failing the matrix.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: API mutating route discovery is generated from a typed route registry or AST pattern that covers route tables, helpers, and switches.
Adding any mutating route without a permission matrix entry fails a targeted test.
The allowed routing patterns are documented and enforced.

proposedDagNode.title: API mutation route registry permission guard
proposedDagNode.summary: Move API mutation permission discovery from current-shape source scanning toward a typed route registry or broader AST guard that catches route-table and helper-based mutations.
deliverables: Typed API mutation route registry or broader AST discovery
Negative fixture for an unregistered mutating route
Docs for allowed API mutation routing patterns
acceptanceCriteria: A new mutating API route without matrix coverage fails a targeted test regardless of current routing helper shape.
Route-table, switch, and helper-predicate mutation forms are either discovered or explicitly rejected.
Existing registered routes keep their denial coverage.
auditFocus: Permission matrix bypass through route refactor

findings.title: DB-backed denial fixtures can skip without database context
findings.description: Permission denial fixtures still skip when DATABASE_URL is unset. That is acceptable for this spec because DB-backed verification was run with a database, but skip visibility should be improved as the CI DB recipes mature.

evidence.detail: Focused audit run passed with skipped DB cases when DATABASE_URL was unset; worker verification passed DB-backed tests with DATABASE_URL.

findings.impact: A local developer can misread a partial permission matrix run as full coverage if skipped DB cases are not surfaced strongly.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Permission matrix test output emits a machine-readable skip artifact or strong diagnostic when DB-backed denial cases are skipped.
CI path for permission matrix runs DB-backed denial cases through the disposable DB recipe.
Docs identify how to run the full permission matrix.

proposedDagNode.title: Permission matrix DB skip visibility
proposedDagNode.summary: Make DB-backed permission denial fixture skips visible and wire the full permission matrix through the disposable DB recipe where appropriate.
deliverables: Machine-readable skip artifact or explicit diagnostic for skipped DB denial fixtures
Docs for full permission matrix execution
CI or recipe linkage to disposable DB-backed permission checks
acceptanceCriteria: Runs without DATABASE_URL clearly report that DB-backed denial coverage was skipped.
The documented full command runs DB-backed permission matrix cases.
No skipped DB matrix run can be confused with full authorization coverage.

findings.title: Permission-call aliasing can evade static discovery
findings.description: The workflow-call and permission-call detectors recognize direct call expressions. Exotic aliasing such as assigning a permission helper to a local variable would not be discovered.

evidence.detail: Current code does not use aliasing, so this is a future hardening gap rather than an active acceptance blocker.

findings.impact: A future developer could accidentally bypass static permission discovery by introducing helper aliases.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Permission helper aliasing is either detected by AST analysis or banned by a targeted test.
Negative fixtures prove aliased requirePermission and requireApiPermission calls fail the guard.
Allowed helper wrappers require explicit annotations and matrix entries.

proposedDagNode.title: Permission helper aliasing guard
proposedDagNode.summary: Harden permission matrix source discovery so aliased permission helpers cannot bypass repository or API mutation denial coverage.
deliverables: AST guard or lint test for aliased permission helper calls
Negative fixtures for aliased repository and API permission calls
Annotation policy for approved permission wrapper helpers
acceptanceCriteria: Aliased requirePermission or requireApiPermission calls fail a targeted test unless explicitly annotated.
Approved wrappers require mutation identity and matrix coverage.
Existing direct permission calls continue to pass.

findings.title: Repository identity is dropped from permission matrix comparison keys
findings.description: Repository source alignment parses repository and method context but compares only sourceFile, mutation, and permission key. Current one-repository-per-file layout keeps this low risk, but future shared files could collapse distinct repository identities.

evidence.detail: Final re-audit found no active incorrect matrix entries, only a future robustness issue.

findings.impact: Future multi-repository files could hide a missing denial fixture or produce confusing matrix diagnostics.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Repository identity is part of the authorization matrix comparison key.
Tests cover two repositories with the same method name and permission key in one file or fixture.
Diagnostics include repository identity for missing or extra permission gates.

proposedDagNode.title: Repository identity in permission matrix keys
proposedDagNode.summary: Include repository identity in repository authorization matrix source-alignment keys and diagnostics so future shared repository files cannot collapse distinct gated mutations.
deliverables: Regression fixture for same method and permission across repositories
acceptanceCriteria: Two repositories with the same method name and permission key require distinct matrix entries.
Missing or extra repository permission gates report repository identity.
